Iran, US review ceasefire plan; Tehran says won't open Hormuz for temporary truceThe US and Iran are exploring a two-step ceasefire framework, but Tehran has refused to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Talks continue amid ongoing regional strikes and President Donald Trump's looming deadline. The proposal, formulated by Pakistan, is being tentatively called as the Islamabad Accord.

Live: 16 Indian-flagged vessels stranded in Strait of Hormuz, says ReutersUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump has escalated tensions with Iran, warning that "power plants and bridges" could be targeted if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday evening, saying "Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one." Trump's abusive threat drew a sharp from Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who warned that Trump's "reckless moves" risk dragging the country into living hell and insisting "you won't gain anything through war crimes." Behind the scenes, Oman has held urgent talks with Iran to ensure smooth flow of transit through war-hit Hormuz, while Pakistan and Egypt have kept diplomatic channels open between Washington and Tehran. At the same time, violence has intensified elsewhere, with Israeli strikes in Beirut killing at least four people and Hezbollah launching retaliatory attacks, escalating the conflict further. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Danger at every step: Airman's rescue mission is red flag for Trump's Iran plansThe US might have succeeded in rescuing a wounded F-15E airman from within Iran, but the risk and cost involved in the mission might be the biggest red flag for President Trump's plan to launch a ground operation. This is why.

Highlights: Arvind Kejriwal seeks court's nod to argue, matter to be heard next weekArvind Kejriwal HC Hearing Highlights: Former Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal argued his case before the Delhi High Court on Monday in connection with the alleged excise policy scam. Kejriwal, along with other accused, has filed a plea seeking the recusal of Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma from hearing the matter. The application has been filed in response to the Enforcement Directorate's petition challenging a trial court order that had acquitted Kejriwal and others in a case linked to alleged non-compliance with summons during the liquor policy probe. In its January 22 ruling, the Rouse Avenue Court had cleared Kejriwal of all the charges, noting that the central probe agency failed to prove any deliberate disobedience of its summons. Stay tuned with indiatoday.in for all live updates.
